title: Quality Management & Outreach Senior Executive (6-Month Contract | SGD 3300 - SGD 3500) – JO 27976
description:
Key Highlights:

  • Flexible Working Hours: Enjoy a structured work schedule with hours from 8:00 AM – 5:30 PM (Tuesday, Thursday to Saturday) and 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM (Wednesday). Working hours may be adjusted based on operational needs.
  • Nearest MRT: Stadium Station
  • Contribute to a robust safety management systems, design engaging learning resources, safety recognition initiatives with supporting large-scale awareness campaigns and cross-functional projects.

Key Responsibilities:

Strategic Quality Assurance

  • Support organisation-level ISO certification and maintenance activities and other accreditation programmes.
  • Maintain and improve quality management systems and documentation to meet statutory and industry requirements.
  • Support safety governance committees and reporting requirements.

Safety Recognition & Culture

  • Develop and implement safety recognition frameworks and awards to strengthen safety ownership.
  • Plan and run staff engagement and culture-building initiatives that reinforce safety as a core value.
  • Build and sustain partnerships with external stakeholders and partners to raise the profile of safety excellence.

Safety Education & Capability Development

  • Review and help improve safety education and training programmes and resources.
  • Contribute to the design of interactive and bite-sized learning solutions (microlearning, guides, immersive content).
  • Coordinate capability-building efforts with subject-matter experts across the sports ecosystem.

Communications & Engagement

  • Support large-scale safety awareness campaigns and national initiatives.
  • Produce safety awareness content for multiple platforms (intranet, website, roadshows).
  • Manage a central knowledge base of safety resources and run targeted outreach to stakeholders.

Division-wide Projects & Partnerships

  • Manage business partnerships related to training and safety for assigned zones/divisions.
  • Support delivery of division-wide events and cross-functional initiatives.
  • Coordinate stakeholders to maximise synergy and impact across teams.


Key Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Sport / Sport Management, Social Science, Workplace Health & Safety, or a related discipline.
  • Relevant experience of 2 years in supporting safety, quality assurance, or programme delivery (preferably within sports, recreation, public agencies, or similar environments).
  • Certifications such as Advanced Certificate in Workplace Safety & Health, ISO 45001 internal auditor, or WSQ modules are a plus.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Excellent written and verbal communications.
  • Effective stakeholder management and ability to work across teams.
  • Proactive, self-motivated, adaptable team player with integrity and a service mindset.


Only shortlisted candidates are notified. Thank you.

By submitting your resume or personal data, you consent to BGC Group Pte Ltd collecting, using, and disclosing your personal data to our clients and partners for the purpose of evaluating your suitability for job opportunities and related recruitment services. You acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to our Privacy Policy for Job Applicants, available at https://bgc-group.com/notice-for-job-applicants.

BGC Group Pte Ltd (Outsourcing)

EA 05C3053

postedCompany.description:
BGC Group is an international recruitment and manpower outsourcing firm that identifies and delivers human capital solutions that imperative to every successful company’s growth. 

Having helped 25,000 individuals quickly land rewarding careers in companies that drive industries since our inception in 2005, we believe in developing relationships based on a solid ground of trust between both employers and job-seekers. This is what differentiates us and gives us a competitive edge from the rest. 

Staying true to our vision of Bridging & Growing Careers Because We Care, we are now proudly listed under Omnibridge Holdings on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange (08462:HK) in 2017.
